system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

# Evolution WhatsApp AI Agent

Deploy a multi-tenant WhatsApp AI support and sales agent. Each client links their WhatsApp Business number → Evolution API instance → webhook to a FastAPI service → AI response back via Evolution.

## Architecture (Upgraded — May 2026)

```
Client WhatsApp Business → Evolution API Instance
    → POST /webhook/{instance} (incoming message)
        → FastAPI router → client system prompt + conversation history
            → LightRAG query (entity-aware memory, port 9623)
                → Hermes-lcm (lossless DAG context, plugin)
                    → AI (DeepSeek → Groq → Ollama fallback chain)
                        → Evolution API /message/sendText → Customer
```

### Hermes Integration

The WhatsApp agent runs alongside Hermes Agent but NOT as a gateway adapter. In Hermes v0.11.0, `whatsapp_provider: evolution` is a no-op — the gateway only supports the built-in Baileys bridge. Evolution runs as a standalone service:

| Component | Role | Port |
|-----------|------|------|
| **Evolution API** | WhatsApp Baileys bridge — message send/receive | 8080 |
| **FastAPI (this service)** | Webhook receiver + client management | 8000 |
| **LightRAG** | Entity-aware long-term memory per contact | 9623 |
| **Hermes-lcm plugin** | Lossless DAG context for long conversations | (plugin, no port) |
| **Skill Factory** | Auto-generates improved sales/support scripts | (plugin, reload on /reset) |
| **Traefik** | Public ingress at whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud | 443 |

### LightRAG Integration (Deployed)

LightRAG runs as a Docker container on port 9623 with its own restart policy and volume persistence. Before each AI response, it's queried for entity-aware context:

```python
rag_url = "http://lightrag:9623"  # Docker network; also reachable as localhost outside container
rag_payload = {
    "query": "what does the customer know about? what products have they asked about?",
    "mode": "hybrid"  # local + global graph search
}
rag_response = requests.post(f"{rag_url}/query", json=rag_payload)
```

**What changed this round (May 5):** Migrated from raw systemd process to Docker container with restart-always policy. LightRAG now survives host reboots. Uses DeepSeek (`deepseek-chat`) as LLM backend (via `LLM_BINDING_HOST` env) and `bge-m3` for 1024-dimensional embeddings via Ollama.

**Ollama runs on host** bound to `0.0.0.0:11434` (not `localhost`) so Docker containers can reach it. Models: `nomic-embed-text` (274MB), `bge-m3`, `qwen2.5-coder:7b`. Ollama is also a service with restart policy.

**Cross-session recall:** "María asked about tent X last week" → "María is now asking about camping stoves — connect the interest."

**LightRAG knowledge base** is seeded on startup via `/seed` endpoint with client's products, FAQs, and policies. Tested query for "devolucion" correctly returns 30-day return policy.

**RAM overhead:** LightRAG + Ollama consumes ~2-4GB total. Ensure the VPS has headroom before starting.

## Stack

| Component | What |
|-----------|------|
| **Evolution API** v2.3.7+ | WhatsApp Baileys bridge — one instance per client phone |
| **FastAPI** webhook receiver | Receives Evolution `onMessage` events, routes per client |
| **Traefik** | Exposes webhook receiver at `whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud` |
| **AI** | DeepSeek (primary), Groq via LiteLLM (free fallback), Ollama (local fallback) |
| **PostgreSQL** | Shared via existing Postgres — Evolution uses it for message storage |

## Deployment

```bash
# Build and deploy (script at /root/whatsapp-ai-agent/deploy.sh)
cd /root/whatsapp-ai-agent
./deploy.sh
```

### Traefik Config

File: `/opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml`

```yaml
http:
  routers:
    whatsapp-ai:
      rule: "Host(`whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud`)"
      entrypoints:
        - websecure
      service: whatsapp-ai
      tls:
        certresolver: letsencrypt
  services:
    whatsapp-ai:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://127.0.0.1:8000"
```

## Client Onboarding

One API call creates the Evolution instance, sets the webhook, and returns a QR code.

```bash
curl -X POST https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "Mi Tienda",
    "phone": "[REDACTED_PHONE]",
    "mode": "ventas"
  }'
```

Response includes a QR code. Client scans with WhatsApp Business app → instance goes `open` → webhook active.

### Modes (built-in prompts)

| Mode | Use |
|------|-----|
| `default` | General customer service |
| `ventas` | Sales assistant — products, pricing, buying process |
| `soporte` | Technical support — returns, warranties, tracking |
| Custom `system_prompt` | Override with any text |

## API Reference

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `/health` | GET | Service status |
| `/clients` | GET | List all clients with history counts |
| `/clients` | POST | Onboard new client (creates Evolution instance + webhook) |
| `/clients/{instance}` | GET | Client details + conversation history |
| `/clients/{instance}` | DELETE | Remove client + delete Evolution instance |
| `/clients/{instance}/qr` | GET | Regenerate QR code for re-pairing |
| `/webhook/{instance}` | POST | Evolution fires this on each incoming message |

### POST /clients payload

```json
{
  "name": "string (required) — client display name",
  "phone": "string (required) — raw digits, no + or spaces",
  "mode": "string (optional) — default|ventas|soporte",
  "system_prompt": "string (optional) — overrides mode",
  "handle_groups": "bool (optional, default false)",
  "webhook_url": "string (optional) — override default webhook target"
}
```

## AI Backend

The service tries providers in order:

1. **DeepSeek** (`deepseek-chat` via `api.deepseek.com/v1`) — $0.14/M input tokens (primary, low latency)
2. **Groq** (`groq-llama` via local LiteLLM proxy on :4000) — free tier, 6 models available, no vision
3. **Ollama** (`qwen2.5-coder:7b` via `host.docker.internal:11434`) — free, always available, runs independently on host
4. Returns error message if all fail

**Fallback chain verification:** All 3 providers tested and confirmed working in this session. LiteLLM/Groq was reconfigured from OpenRouter to reduce costs. Cron jobs migrated from OpenRouter/Anthropic to DeepSeek.

Before each AI call, the service queries LightRAG for entity-aware context about the customer and conversation topic. This context is prepended to the system prompt. The LightRAG knowledge base is pre-seeded with client products, FAQs, and policies.

Conversation history is stored per client (in-memory dict, last 50 messages). Postgres persistence is planned.

### Session Context Chain (Order of Operations)

1. Receive webhook message
2. Filter `fromMe: true` messages (echo prevention)
3. Check conversation history for this remoteJid
4. Query LightRAG for relevant entity context
5. Build prompt: system_prompt + RAG context + conversation history + new message
6. Call AI (try DeepSeek → Groq → Ollama fallback chain)
7. Send response via Evolution API sendText
8. Store message + response in conversation history
9. Optionally update LightRAG with new entities from the exchange

## Pitfalls

1. **Evolution instance creation requires `integration: WHATSAPP-BAILEYS`** — without it the API returns `400 Invalid integration`
2. **Webhook URL must be publicly reachable** — Evolution runs in Docker overlay, can't reach `localhost`. Must use Traefik public URL (e.g. `[REDACTED_WEBHOOK] **QR codes expire in ~45 seconds** — be ready to scan immediately. Regenerate with `GET /clients/{instance}/qr`
4. **WhatsApp max 4 linked devices** per phone number. Client may need to remove old links
### Hermes Upgrades Applied This Session

Also applied via the `hermes-full-upgrade` skill:
- Removed stale OpenRouter credential pool entries
- Reconfigured auxiliary models from OpenRouter to LiteLLM/Groq (free, 6 models available)
- Added Ollama (`qwen2.5-coder:7b`) as local fallback provider
- All cron jobs migrated from OpenRouter/Anthropic/Claude to DeepSeek/deepseek-chat
- Skills pruned from 196 to 56
- **hermes-lcm plugin** enabled (lossless context for long conversations)
- **Skill Factory** installed as meta-skill (auto-generates skills from workflows)
- Deployed integration documentation to 21 client websites (`*whatsapp-ai-integration.md`)

## Research-Based Upgrade Roadmap

See `references/evolution-upgrade-research-2026-05.md` for full findings. High-priority next builds:

1. **LightRAG** — ✓ DEPLOYED: entity-aware memory across sessions (Docker on :9623, query before each AI call)
2. **Custom Business MCP** — product/FAQ/orders lookup tools for the AI to call during conversations
3. **Session persistence** — in-memory dict → Postgres (already running)
4. **SOUL.md personalities** — proven formula for sales/support/default personas
5. **Supabase MCP** — real-time product/order queries for AI
6. **Langfuse observability** — conversation tracing, cost analytics (pending)

Here's the plan:

**WhatsApp has 43,741 messages from 409 contacts** (hermes-whatsapp instance). The messages are already stored in Evolution's Postgres. We need to:

1. **Add an import endpoint** to the whatsapp-ai agent that fetches messages from Evolution's Postgres and ingests them into LightRAG
2. **Add a message history API** so the AI agent can read past conversations per contact
3. **Run the batch import** to feed all past data into LightRAG

Let me build this. The core idea: give the agent the Postgres connection so it can query past messages directly, and batch-import historical conversations into LightRAG.

function.name: write_file
function.arguments:
{"path": "/root/whatsapp-ai-agent/app/main.py", "content": """"
WhatsApp AI Agent v2.1 — Multi-Client Business Chatbot
- LightRAG long-term memory with past-conversation import
- Postgres message history (reads Evolution's DB directly)
- Past-chat import via POST /clients/{instance}/import
- Chat history API per contact
- Business MCP tools (products, FAQ, policies)
- SOUL.md personality profiles
- Supabase integration
"""
import os, json, logging, asyncio

# ── Client Store (Postgres-backed) ──
clients: dict[str, dict] = {}

def _evo_headers() -> dict:
    return {"apikey": EVO_KEY, "Content-Type": "application/json"}

# ── Postgres Connection ──
_pg_pool: Optional[asyncpg.Pool] = None

async def _get_pg() -> asyncpg.Pool:
    global _pg_pool
    if _pg_pool is None:
        _pg_pool = await asyncpg.create_pool(PG_DSN, min_size=2, max_size=5)
    return _pg_pool

async def _get_evolution_instance_id(name: str) -> str | None:
    """Get Evolution instance DB ID by instance name."""
    pool = await _get_pg()
    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
        row = await conn.fetchrow(
            'SELECT id FROM "Instance" WHERE name = $1', name
        )
        return row["id"] if row else None

async def _fetch_chat_messages(instance_name: str, remote_jid: str, limit: int = 100) -> list[dict]:
    """Fetch message history for a specific WhatsApp contact from Evolution's DB."""
    pool = await _get_pg()
    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
        inst_id = await conn.fetchrow(
            'SELECT id FROM "Instance" WHERE name = $1', instance_name
        )
        if not inst_id:
            return []
        rows = await conn.fetch(
            """SELECT key, message, "messageType", "messageTimestamp", pushName
            FROM "Message"
            WHERE "instanceId" = $1 AND key->>'remoteJid' = $2
            ORDER BY "messageTimestamp" DESC
            LIMIT $3""",
            inst_id["id"], remote_jid, limit,
        )
        result = []
        for r in rows:
            key = r["key"] if isinstance(r["key"], dict) else json.loads(r["key"])
            msg = r["message"] if isinstance(r["message"], dict) else json.loads(r["message"])
            result.append({
                "from_me": key.get("fromMe", False),
                "remote_jid": key.get("remoteJid", ""),
                "type": r["messageType"],
                "timestamp": r["messageTimestamp"],
                "text": msg.get("conversation", "") or msg.get("extendedTextMessage", {}).get("text", ""),
                "push_name": r["pushName"] or "",
            })
        return result

async def _fetch_all_chats(instance_name: str) -> list[dict]:
    """Get all chats with their message counts and date ranges."""
    pool = await _get_pg()
    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
        inst_id = await conn.fetchrow(
            'SELECT id FROM "Instance" WHERE name = $1', instance_name
        )
        if not inst_id:
            return []
        rows = await conn.fetch(
            """SELECT
                key->>'remoteJid' as remote_jid,
                COUNT(*) as msg_count,
                MIN("messageTimestamp") as first_ts,
                MAX("messageTimestamp") as last_ts,
                BOOL_OR((key->>'fromMe')::boolean) as has_outgoing
            FROM "Message"
            WHERE "instanceId" = $1
            GROUP BY remote_jid
            ORDER BY msg_count DESC
            LIMIT 200""",
            inst_id["id"],
        )
        return [{
            "remote_jid": r["remote_jid"],
            "msg_count": r["msg_count"],
            "first_message": r["first_ts"],
            "last_message": r["last_ts"],
            "has_outgoing": r["has_outgoing"],
        } for r in rows]

async def _enrich_chat_name(remote_jid: str) -> str:
    """Try to get a pushName for a remoteJid from latest message."""
    pool = await _get_pg()
    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
        row = await conn.fetchrow(
            """SELECT pushName FROM "Message"
            WHERE key->>'remoteJid' = $1 AND pushName IS NOT NULL
            ORDER BY "messageTimestamp" DESC LIMIT 1""",
            remote_jid
        )
        return row["pushname"] if row else remote_jid.split("@")[0]

# ── LightRAG Client ──
async def _lightrag_query(query: str, customer_id: str = "") -> str:
    """Query the knowledge graph for context about a customer or topic."""
    enriched = f"{query} (cliente: {customer_id})" if customer_id else query
    try:
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10) as cl:
            resp = await cl.post(
                f"{LIGHTRAG}/query",
                json={"query": enriched, "mode": "hybrid", "only_need_context": True},
            )
            if resp.status_code == 200:
                return resp.json().get("context", resp.json().get("response", ""))
    except Exception as e:
        log.warning(f"LightRAG query failed: {e}")
    return ""

async def _lightrag_ingest(text: str, source: str = "conversation"):
    """Store a conversation summary into the knowledge graph."""
    try:
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as cl:
            await cl.post(
                f"{LIGHTRAG}/documents/text",
                json={"text": text, "description": source},
            )
    except Exception as e:
        log.warning(f"LightRAG ingest failed: {e}")

async def _lightrag_ingest_messages(messages: list[dict], contact: str, instance: str):
    """Batch-ingest multiple conversation snippets to LightRAG."""
    # Group by conversation flows (max 20 messages per chunk)
    chunks = []
    chunk = []
    for m in reversed(messages):  # chronological order
        prefix = "Yo" if m["from_me"] else "Cliente"
        chunk.append(f"{prefix}: {m['text']}")
        if len(chunk) >= 20:
            chunks.append(chunk)
            chunk = []
    if chunk:
        chunks.append(chunk)

    for i, c in enumerate(chunks):
        text = "\n".join(c)
        await _lightrag_ingest(
            text,
            source=f"chat_history:{instance}:{contact}:batch_{i}",
        )
    return len(chunks)

# ── AI Chat with Memory ──
async def _ai_chat(profile: str, history: list, user_msg: str, customer_id: str = "",
                   instance: str = "") -> str:
    """Call AI with: system prompt + memory context + past history."""
    system = profile

    # Inject LightRAG context
    rag_context = await _lightrag_query(user_msg, customer_id)
    if rag_context:
        system += f"\n\nContexto histórico relevante:\n{rag_context[:1500]}"

    # Fetch Evolution message history for additional context
    if instance and customer_id:
        past_msgs = await _fetch_chat_messages(instance, f"{customer_id}@s.whatsapp.net", 30)
        if past_msgs:
            past_text = "\n".join(
                f"{'Yo' if m['from_me'] else 'Cliente'}: {m['text']}"
                for m in reversed(past_msgs[-15:])
            )
            system += f"\n\nHistorial de conversación (Evolution DB):\n{past_text[:2000]}"

    messages = [{"role": "system", "content": system}]
    for h in history[-MAX_HIST:]:
        messages.append(h)
    messages.append({"role": "user", "content": user_msg})

    # Try DeepSeek first, then LiteLLM fallback
    for name, cfg in [
        ("deepseek", {"url": DS_URL, "key": DS_KEY, "model": DS_MODEL}),
        ("litellm",  {"url": LL_URL, "key": LL_KEY, "model": "groq-llama"}),
    ]:
        if not cfg["key"]:
            continue
        try:
            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30) as cl:
                resp = await cl.post(
                    f"{cfg['url']}/chat/completions",
                    json={"model": cfg["model"], "messages": messages,
                          "max_tokens": 1024, "temperature": 0.7},
                    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {cfg['key']}",
                             "Content-Type": "application/json"},
                )
                if resp.status_code == 200:
                    data = resp.json()
                    return data["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
                log.warning(f"{name} returned {resp.status_code}: {resp.text[:200]}")
        except Exception as e:
            log.warning(f"{name} failed: {e}")
    return "Lo siento, tengo problemas técnicos. Por favor intenta de nuevo."

async def _evo_send(instance: str, number: str, text: str):
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=15) as cl:
        await cl.post(
            f"{EVO_URL}/message/sendText/{instance}",
            json={"number": number, "text": text, "delay": REPLY_MS},
            headers=_evo_headers(),
        )

# ── FastAPI App ──

@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    log.info("WhatsApp AI Agent v2.1 starting...")
    log.info(f"LightRAG: {'✓' if LIGHTRAG else '✗'}")
    log.info(f"DeepSeek: {'✓' if DS_KEY else '✗'}")
    log.info(f"Postgres: {'✓' if PG_DSN else '✗'}")
    log.info(f"Profiles loaded: {list(SOUL_PROFILES.keys())}")
    yield
    global _pg_pool
    if _pg_pool:
        await _pg_pool.close()
    log.info("WhatsApp AI Agent v2.1 stopped.")

app = FastAPI(title="WhatsApp AI Agent v2.1", version="2.1.0", lifespan=lifespan)

# ── Webhook ──
\[REDACTED_EMAIL]("/webhook/{instance}")
async def webhook(instance: str, request: Request):
    body = await request.json()
    log.info(f"Webhook from {instance}: event type = {body.get('event','message')}")

    client = clients.get(instance)
    if not client:
        return {"status": "ignored", "reason": "unknown_instance"}

    data = body.get("data", body)
    key = data.get("key", {})
    remote = key.get("remoteJid", "")
    msg_data = data.get("message", data)
    text = ""
    for field in ["conversation", "extendedTextMessage", "imageMessage", "videoMessage"]:
        if field in msg_data:
            if isinstance(msg_data[field], dict):
                text = msg_data[field].get("text", "") or msg_data[field].get("caption", "")
            else:
                text = msg_data[field]
            break

    sender = remote.split("@")[0]
    if key.get("fromMe", False) or "@g.us" in remote:
        return {"status": "ignored"}
    if not text.strip():
        return {"status": "ignored"}

    log.info(f"[{client['name']}] {sender}: {text[:100]}")

    client.setdefault("history", []).append({"role": "user", "content": text})

    profile = SOUL_PROFILES.get(client.get("mode", "default"), SOUL_PROFILES["default"])
    response = await _ai_chat(profile, client["history"], text,
                              customer_id=sender, instance=instance)

    client["history"].append({"role": "assistant", "content": response})
    await _evo_send(instance, sender, response)
    log.info(f"Replied to {sender}: {response[:80]}...")

    return {"status": "ok"}

Option 2 is simpler — just add `ports: "8000:8000"` to docker-compose.

Traefik has BOTH `swarm` and `docker` providers. The `docker` provider uses the container labels, and `swarm` uses Swarm labels. But also `providers.file.directory` loads from `/etc/traefik/dynamic`. The static file config at `/opt/traefik/dynamic/whatsapp-ai.yml` defines a service pointing to `127.0.0.1:8000`, which overrides the swarm auto-discovery.

I need to remove that file config and let the Docker Swarm labels handle it.

docker service inspect whatsapp-ai-stack_whatsapp-ai --format '{{range $k,$v := .Spec.Labels}}{{$k}}={{$v}}{{"\n"}}{{end}}' 2>/dev/null
echo "---"
# Also check the container labels
docker ps --filter name=whatsapp-ai-stack --format '{{.Labels}}' | head -1 2>/dev/null | tr ',' '\n' | grep -i traefik", "timeout": 10}

But the old file config was pointing to `127.0.0.1:8000` — now that file is removed. Since Traefik uses `providers.file.watch=true`, it should detect the file was removed and update its routing.

## Summary — WhatsApp History Import v2.1.0 Deployed

### What was done

**WhatsApp AI Agent upgraded to v2.1.0** with 3 new APIs:

| Endpoint | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `GET /clients/{instance}/chats` | List all contacts with message counts & date ranges |
| `GET /clients/{instance}/messages/{jid}` | Get past messages for a specific contact |
| `POST /clients/{instance}/import` | Import past chats into LightRAG memory |

**Key architectural change:** Agent now connects directly to Evolution's Postgres (43,741 messages, 409 contacts, back to June 2024) instead of depending on Evolution API message endpoints, which are unavailable.

### Status

- **Service running** at `whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud` (v2.1.0, Postgres ✓, DeepSeek ✓, LightRAG ✓)
- **Import endpoint works** but last deploy had a `pushName` column quoting fix that didn't propagate because the container image wasn't refreshed (same tag `v2.1.0`). The fix is in the source at `/root/whatsapp-ai-agent/app/main.py:177` (`row["pushName"]`).
- **Need to bump the image tag** (e.g. `v2.1.2`) and redeploy to get the fix applied.

### To run the full import after redeploy

```bash
# Import ALL individual chats (top 50 chats, 500 messages each)
curl -X POST https://whatsapp-ai.sunstein.cloud/clients/hermes-whatsapp/import \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"max_per_chat": 500, "max_chats": 50}'
```

This will feed ~25,000 messages across 50 contacts into LightRAG as entity-aware memory chunks. After that, every new WhatsApp message will include past conversation context from that contact.
can you send whatsapp messages?

Evolution API v2.3.7 on this server only has these instance endpoints — no pairing code API. The QR is the only connection method.

The "can't link device" error usually means one of:
1. The QR already expired (45s window)
2. WhatsApp Web session limit reached (max 4 linked devices)
3. The number already has 4 active sessions

Let me generate a fresh QR and save it:

function.name: terminal
function.arguments:
{"command": "HERMES_TOKEN="5C[REDACTED_PHONE]DE-407B-BF96-FFC1D84A79E3"

# Logout and get fresh QR
curl -s -X DELETE "https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/instance/logout/hermes-whatsapp" \
  -H "apikey: $HERMES_TOKEN" 2>/dev/null > /dev/null

sleep 2

curl -s "https://evolution.sunstein.cloud/instance/connect/hermes-whatsapp" \
  -H "apikey: $HERMES_TOKEN" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys,json,base64
d=json.load(sys.stdin)
b64 = d.get('base64','')
if ',' in b64: b64 = b64.split(',')[1]
with open('/root/hermes-qr.png','wb') as f:
    f.write(base64.b64decode(b64))
print('Fresh QR saved')
" 2>/dev/null

echo "Done - fresh QR at /root/hermes-qr.png"", "timeout": 15}

content: {"output": "Fresh QR saved\nDone - fresh QR at /root/hermes-qr.png", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

- Settings (gear icon) > Linked devices > Link a device

If it still says "can't link", you may need to unlink an old device first:

what should we do next?

The hermes-whatsapp QR is the bottleneck. Once it's scanned:

**After QR scanned (automatic):**
- 2 pending messages to Luana deliver
- AI agent starts responding to incoming customer messages
- All 8,610 past messages are in LightRAG — AI has full context

**After Luana scans her QR:**
- Her instance connects
- AI handles her conversations too
- All her past messages already imported

**Other things ready to do in parallel:**
- Full import of ALL 43,741 messages (we only did top 50 chats)
- Langfuse observability setup
- Set up the other contact instances

Sent the Evolution Manager screenshot with the QR visible to your Telegram.

Now about the **bigger plan** — here's the architecture I recommend for client onboarding:

**Architecture:**

```
Client signs up → gets assigned:
  ├─ Evolution instance (their WhatsApp Business)
  ├─ Supabase tenant (products, orders, FAQ)
  ├─ Knowledge base (their docs/site)
  └─ AI personality profile (sales/support/tone)

WhatsApp customer messages → Evolution webhook
  → whatsapp-ai-agent (v2.1)
    → LightRAG (client-specific memory)
    → AI (DeepSeek)
    → Reply via Evolution
```

**Client Whatsapp setup (simplified):**
1. Client clicks link → opens manager.evolution.sunstein.cloud
2. OR we send them a QR → they scan with WhatsApp Business
3. OR we use **pairing code** (8-digit, no QR) — this is what's missing from v2.3.7

**To fix:**
- The Manager UI works with the **full API key**: `[REDACTED_TOKEN]`
- But v2.3.7 doesn't support pairing codes. We'd need Evolution API v2.4+ or a different approach.

**Two approaches:**
1. **Upgrade Evolution** to latest (has WhatsApp Business API pairing code — no QR scan needed, client just enters 8-digit code)
2. **Self-serve portal** — a simple webpage where clients get a QR and scan it

Which path do you want? If we go with option 1, I'll research what version supports pairing codes and upgrade.

    96|async def _get_evolution_instance_id(name: str) -> str | None:
    97|    """Get Evolution instance DB ID by instance name."""
    98|    pool = await _get_pg()
    99|    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
   100|        row = await conn.fetchrow(
   101|            'SELECT id FROM "Instance" WHERE name = $1', name
   102|        )
   103|        return row["id"] if row else None

   105|async def _fetch_chat_messages(instance_name: str, remote_jid: str, limit: int = 100) -> list[dict]:
   106|    """Fetch message history for a specific WhatsApp contact from Evolution's DB."""
   107|    pool = await _get_pg()
   108|    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
   109|        inst_id = await conn.fetchrow(
   110|            'SELECT id FROM "Instance" WHERE name = $1', instance_name
   111|        )
   112|        if not inst_id:
   113|            return []
   114|        rows = await conn.fetch(
   115|            """SELECT key, message, "messageType", "messageTimestamp", "pushName"
   116|            FROM "Message"
   117|            WHERE "instanceId" = $1 AND key->>'remoteJid' = $2
   118|            ORDER BY "messageTimestamp" DESC
   119|            LIMIT $3""",
   120|            inst_id["id"], remote_jid, limit,
   121|        )
   122|        result = []
   123|        for r in rows:
   124|            key = r["key"] if isinstance(r["key"], dict) else json.loads(r["key"])
   125|            msg = r["message"] if isinstance(r["message"], dict) else json.loads(r["message"])
   126|            result.append({
   127|                "from_me": key.get("fromMe", False),
   128|                "remote_jid": key.get("remoteJid", ""),
   129|                "type": r["messageType"],
   130|                "timestamp": r["messageTimestamp"],
   131|                "text": msg.get("conversation", "") or msg.get("extendedTextMessage", {}).get("text", ""),
   132|                "push_name": r["pushName"] or "",
   133|            })
   134|        return result
   135|
   136|async def _fetch_all_chats(instance_name: str) -> list[dict]:
   137|    """Get all chats with their message counts and date ranges."""
   138|    pool = await _get_pg()
   139|    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
   140|        inst_id = await conn.fetchrow(
   141|            'SELECT id FROM "Instance" WHERE name = $1', instance_name
   142|        )
   143|        if not inst_id:
   144|            return []
   145|        rows = await conn.fetch(
   146|            """SELECT
   147|                key->>'remoteJid' as remote_jid,
   148|                COUNT(*) as msg_count,
   149|                MIN("messageTimestamp") as first_ts,
   150|                MAX("messageTimestamp") as last_ts,
   151|                BOOL_OR((key->>'fromMe')::boolean) as has_outgoing
   152|            FROM "Message"
   153|            WHERE "instanceId" = $1
   154|            GROUP BY remote_jid
   155|            ORDER BY msg_count DESC
   156|            LIMIT 200""",
   157|            inst_id["id"],
   158|        )
   159|        return [{
   160|            "remote_jid": r["remote_jid"],
   161|            "msg_count": r["msg_count"],
   162|            "first_message": r["first_ts"],
   163|            "last_message": r["last_ts"],
   164|            "has_outgoing": r["has_outgoing"],
   165|        } for r in rows]
   166|
   167|async def _enrich_chat_name(remote_jid: str) -> str:
   168|    """Try to get a pushName for a remoteJid from latest message."""
   169|    pool = await _get_pg()
   170|    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
   171|        row = await conn.fetchrow(
   172|            """SELECT "pushName" FROM "Message"
   173|            WHERE key->>'remoteJid' = $1 AND "pushName" IS NOT NULL
   174|            ORDER BY "messageTimestamp" DESC LIMIT 1""",
   175|            remote_jid
   176|        )
   177|        return row["pushName"] if row else remote_jid.split("@")[0]

   181|    """Query the knowledge graph for context about a customer or topic."""

   196|    """Store a conversation summary into the knowledge graph."""

   206|async def _lightrag_ingest_messages(messages: list[dict], contact: str, instance: str):
   207|    """Batch-ingest multiple conversation snippets to LightRAG."""
   208|    # Group by conversation flows (max 20 messages per chunk)
   209|    chunks = []
   210|    chunk = []
   211|    for m in reversed(messages):  # chronological order
   212|        prefix = "Yo" if m["from_me"] else "Cliente"
   213|        chunk.append(f"{prefix}: {m['text']}")
   214|        if len(chunk) >= 20:
   215|            chunks.append(chunk)
   216|            chunk = []
   217|    if chunk:
   218|        chunks.append(chunk)
   219|
   220|    for i, c in enumerate(chunks):
   221|        text = "\n".join(c)
   222|        await _lightrag_ingest(
   223|            text,
   224|            source=f"chat_history:{instance}:{contact}:batch_{i}",
   225|        )
   226|    return len(chunks)

   244|async def _ai_chat(profile: str, history: list, user_msg: str, customer_id: str = "",

   246|    """Call AI with: system prompt + memory context + past history."""
   247|    system = profile

   251|    if rag_context:

   253|
   254|    # Fetch Evolution message history for additional context
   255|    if instance and customer_id:
   256|        past_msgs = await _fetch_chat_messages(instance, f"{customer_id}@s.whatsapp.net", 30)
   257|        if past_msgs:
   258|            past_text = "\n".join(
   259|                f"{'Yo' if m['from_me'] else 'Cliente'}: {m['text']}"

   262|            system += f"\n\nHistorial de conversación (Evolution DB):\n{past_text[:2000]}"
   263|
   264|    messages = [{"role": "system", "content": system}]
   265|    for h in history[-MAX_HIST:]:
   266|        messages.append(h)

   268|
   269|    # Try DeepSeek first, then LiteLLM fallback

   272|        ("litellm",  {"url": LL_URL, "key": LL_KEY, "model": "groq-llama"}),

   289|        except Exception as e:

Sort by recency, skip groups, take top N 513| sorted_chats = sorted(all_chats, key=lambda c: c["last_message"], reverse=True) 514| individual = [c for c in sorted_chats if "@g.us" not in c["remote_jid"]] 515| chats_to_import = individual[:req.max_chats] 516| 517| total_chunks = 0 518| total_messages = 0 519| results = [] 520| 521| for chat in chats_to_import: 522| try: 523| msgs = await _fetch_chat_messages(instance, chat["remote_jid"], req.max_per_chat) 524| if not msgs: 525| continue 526| contact_name = msgs[0].get("push_name") or msgs[0]["remote_jid"].split("@")[0] 527| chunks = await _lightrag_ingest_messages(msgs, chat["remote_jid"], instance) 528| total_chunks += chunks 529| total_messages += len(msgs) 530| results.append({ 531| "remote_jid": chat["remote_jid"], 532| "name": contact_name, 533| "messages_imported": len(msgs), 534| "lightrag_chunks": chunks, 535| }) 536| except Exception as e: 537| log.warning(f"Import failed for {chat['remote_jid']}: {e}") 538| results.append({ 539| "remote_jid": chat["remote_jid"], 540| "error": str(e), 541| }) 542| 543| return { 544| "status": "done", 545| "instance": instance, 546| "chats_imported": len(results), 547| "total_messages": total_messages, 548| "total_lightrag_chunks": total_chunks, 549| "results": results, 550| } 551| 552|# ── Health & Root ── 553| 554|@app.get("/health") 555|async def health(): 556| pg_ok = False 557| try: 558| pool = await _get_pg() 559| async with pool.acquire() as conn: 560| await conn.fetchval("SELECT 1") 561| pg_ok = True 562| except Exception as e: 563| log.warning(f"Health PG check failed: {e}") 564| pass 565| return { 566| "status": "ok", 567| "version": "2.1.0", 568| "clients_active": len(clients), 569| "lightrag": bool(LIGHTRAG), 570| "deepseek": bool(DS_KEY), 571| "postgres": pg_ok, 572| "profiles": list(SOUL_PROFILES.keys()), 573| } 574| 575|@app.get("/", response_class=HTMLResponse) 576|async def root(): 577| return """ 578|

⚕ WhatsApp AI Agent v2.1

579|

Multi-client WhatsApp Business: support + sales + memory + tools + past-history.

580|

Stack: DeepSeek + LightRAG + Postgres + Evolution + SOUL profiles

581|

/health

582|

New APIs

583|
   584|GET  /clients/{instance}/chats              — List all chats with message counts
   585|GET  /clients/{instance}/messages/{jid}     — Get past messages for a contact
   586|POST /clients/{instance}/import             — Import past chats into LightRAG
587|

Import past conversations:

588|
POST /clients/hermes-whatsapp/import
   589|{
   590|  "contact_jid": null,      // null = all chats
   591|  "max_per_chat": 500,
   592|  "max_chats": 50
   593|}
594|""" 595| 596|if __name__ == "__main__": 597| import uvicorn 598| uvicorn.run("app.main:app", host=HOST, port=PORT, reload=True) 599|", "total_lines": 598, "file_size": 23606, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

""", timeout=30)
print(r["output"])
"}

function.arguments:
{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal

# Search for the evolution docker-compose/stack file
r = terminal("""
# Find evolution's config
docker service inspect evolution_evolution_api --format '{{json .Spec.TaskTemplate.ContainerSpec}}' 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys,json
spec=json.load(sys.stdin)
print('Image:', spec.get('Image','?'))
print('Env count:', len(spec.get('Env',[])))
for e in spec.get('Env',[]):
    if 'KEY' in e or 'TOKEN' in e or 'SECRET' in e or 'PASSWORD' in e:
        parts=e.split('=',1)
        print(f'  {parts[0]}=***')
    else:
        print(f'  {e}')
" 2>/dev/null

echo ""
echo "=== Find evolution stack file ==="
find /root /opt /etc /var -maxdepth 4 -name "*.yml" -o -name "*.yaml" 2>/dev/null | xargs grep -l "evolution" 2>/dev/null | head -10
""", timeout=10)
print(r["output"])
"}

---

# Complete System Analysis — WhatsApp AI Agent Stack

## 1. Architecture Overview

```
CUSTOMER → WhatsApp → Evolution API v2.3.7 (Baileys bridge)
                         ↓ Webhook
                    whatsapp-ai-agent v2.1.0 (FastAPI :8000)
                         ↓
                    ┌───────┼────────┐
                    ↓       ↓        ↓
               DeepSeek  LightRAG  Postgres
               (primary) (memory)  (43k msgs)
                    ↓
               LiteLLM/Groq (fallback)
                    ↓
               Reply → Evolution API → Customer WhatsApp
```

## 2. What's Working

| Component | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| **Evolution API** | ✓ v2.3.7 | 2 instances, 43,747 msgs stored |
| **whatsapp-ai-agent** | ✓ v2.1.0 | 3 SOUL profiles, webhook receiver |
| **LightRAG** | ✓ v1.4.15 | DeepSeek + bge-m3 embeddings + 8,610 msgs imported |
| **DeepSeek** | ✓ | Primary AI, $0.14/M tokens |
| **LiteLLM/Groq** | ✓ | Free fallback, 6 models |
| **Postgres** | ✓ | Shared, Evolution DB connected |
| **Evolution Manager UI** | ✓ | Login at /manager with full API key |
| **Past-data import** | ✓ | 50 chats, 8,610 msgs → LightRAG |
| **Chat history API** | ✓ | GET /chats, GET /messages/{jid} |
| **QR generation** | ✓ | Via GET /clients/{instance}/qr |
| **Traefik ingress** | ✓ | 2 subdomains: evolution.*, whatsapp-ai.* |

## 3. Critical Gaps

### A. WhatsApp Connection (BLOCKER)
- **No pairing code support** — Evolution v2.3.7 only supports QR code scanning
- QR codes expire in 45s, hard to use in a client onboarding flow
- The `hermes-whatsapp` instance is `open` but messages stay `PENDING` — connection needs manual QR re-scan
- **Fix needed**: Upgrade to Evolution API v2.4+ or use WhatsApp Business Cloud API for pairing codes
- **Reality**: The repo hasn't been updated in 5 months. v2.3.7 IS the latest

### B. Client-Repo Mapping (NOT BUILT)
- No concept of "which WhatsApp contact belongs to which client repo"
- 409 contacts exist in Evolution DB but aren't mapped to any business/store
- **Need**: A `client_repos` table mapping: `Evolution instance → remoteJid → client repo → AI personality`

### C. Per-Client Context Isolation (NOT BUILT)
- LightRAG currently mixes all conversations into one global graph
- Different stores (Nexa, El Viajero, Superspuma, etc.) share the same memory
- **Need**: Tenant isolation in LightRAG or separate LightRAG instances per client

### D. Self-Service Client Onboarding (NOT BUILT)
- Clients currently need us to create an instance and send a QR
- **Need**: A web portal where clients:
  1. Sign up → get an Evolution instance created
  2. See a QR code on screen (refreshes every 45s)
  3. Scan with WhatsApp Business → instance connects
  4. Choose their store/repo from a dropdown
  5. AI personality auto-assigns based on store

### E. AI Tool Execution (PARTIAL)
- `BUSINESS_TOOLS` defined but NEVER called in the AI flow
- The `_ai_chat` function doesn't do tool-calling — it just sends a system prompt
- **Need**: Proper function-calling with DeepSeek or switch to OpenAI-compatible tool calling

### F. Message History Import (PARTIAL)

Review the conversation above and update the skill library. Be ACTIVE — most sessions produce at least one skill update, even if small. A pass that does nothing is a missed learning opportunity, not a neutral outcome.

Target shape of the library: CLASS-LEVEL skills, each with a rich SKILL.md and a `references/` directory for session-specific detail. Not a long flat list of narrow one-session-one-skill entries. This shapes HOW you update, not WHETHER you update.

Signals to look for (any one of these warrants action):
  • User corrected your style, tone, format, legibility, or verbosity. Frustration signals like 'stop doing X', 'this is too verbose', 'don't format like this', 'why are you explaining', 'just give me the answer', 'you always do Y and I hate it', or an explicit 'remember this' are FIRST-CLASS skill signals, not just memory signals. Update the relevant skill(s) to embed the preference so the next session starts already knowing.
  • User corrected your workflow, approach, or sequence of steps. Encode the correction as a pitfall or explicit step in the skill that governs that class of task.
  • Non-trivial technique, fix, workaround, debugging path, or tool-usage pattern emerged that a future session would benefit from. Capture it.
  • A skill that got loaded or consulted this session turned out to be wrong, missing a step, or outdated. Patch it NOW.

Preference order — prefer the earliest action that fits, but do pick one when a signal above fired:
  1. UPDATE A CURRENTLY-LOADED SKILL. Look back through the conversation for skills the user loaded via /skill-name or you read via skill_view. If any of them covers the territory of the new learning, PATCH that one first. It is the skill that was in play, so it's the right one to extend.
  2. UPDATE AN EXISTING UMBRELLA (via skills_list + skill_view). If no loaded skill fits but an existing class-level skill does, patch it. Add a subsection, a pitfall, or broaden a trigger.
  3. ADD A SUPPORT FILE under an existing umbrella. Skills can be packaged with three kinds of support files — use the right directory per kind:
     • `references/<topic>.md` — session-specific detail (error transcripts, reproduction recipes, provider quirks) AND condensed knowledge banks: quoted research, API docs, external authoritative excerpts, or domain notes you found while working on the problem. Write it concise and for the value of the task, not as a full mirror of upstream docs.
     • `templates/<name>.<ext>` — starter files meant to be copied and modified (boilerplate configs, scaffolding, a known-good example the agent can `reproduce with modifications`).
     • `scripts/<name>.<ext>` — statically re-runnable actions the skill can invoke directly (verification scripts, fixture generators, deterministic probes, anything the agent should run rather than hand-type each time).
     Add support files via skill_manage action=write_file with file_path starting 'references/', 'templates/', or 'scripts/'. The umbrella's SKILL.md should gain a one-line pointer to any new support file so future agents know it exists.
  4. CREATE A NEW CLASS-LEVEL UMBRELLA SKILL when no existing skill covers the class. The name MUST be at the class level. The name MUST NOT be a specific PR number, error string, feature codename, library-alone name, or 'fix-X / debug-Y / audit-Z-today' session artifact. If the proposed name only makes sense for today's task, it's wrong — fall back to (1), (2), or (3).

User-preference embedding (important): when the user expressed a style/format/workflow preference, the update belongs in the SKILL.md body, not just in memory. Memory captures 'who the user is and what the current situation and state of your operations are'; skills capture 'how to do this class of task for this user'. When they complain about how you handled a task, the skill that governs that task needs to carry the lesson.

If you notice two existing skills that overlap, note it in your reply — the background curator handles consolidation at scale.

'Nothing to save.' is a real option but should NOT be the default. If the session ran smoothly with no corrections and produced no new technique, just say 'Nothing to save.' and stop. Otherwise, act.
